title: FsDelete
description: fs.delete — remove a file or directory. When recursive=false (default) on a directory that contains children, the operation fails with fs.not_empty. When recursive=true, the directory and all its contents are removed (rm -rf semantics). Deleting a non-existent path returns fs.not_found so callers can distinguish 'already gone' from success.

path.description: Workspace-relative path to delete (file or directory).

recursive.description: If true and path is a directory, delete the entire subtree.

path.description: The path that was deleted (echo of request).
